speaker: rep
text: Hi, is this Alex? This is Jamie from RepReady. I hope I'm not catching you at a bad time.

speaker: prospect
text: Uh, yeah this is Alex. I'm a bit busy actually. What's this about?

speaker: rep
text: I'll be brief. I noticed your company just raised a Series B and you're scaling your sales team. Congrats on the round.

speaker: prospect
text: Thanks. Yeah, we're hiring a bunch of SDRs right now. But I get a lot of these calls.

speaker: rep
text: Totally fair. The reason I'm reaching out is that we work with a lot of Series B companies that are scaling from 5 to 20 reps, and the biggest challenge they hit is keeping outbound quality high while ramping new hires.

speaker: prospect
text: That's actually spot on. Our new hires take about 3 months to ramp and their reply rates are terrible in the beginning.

speaker: rep
text: Yeah so basically what we do is we have this AI coaching platform that analyzes live sales calls and gives real-time feedback to reps and it also does post-call scorecards and it has a practice mode where reps can simulate calls and it tracks improvement over time and it integrates with Gong and Chorus and Salesforce and it has a manager dashboard with team analytics and individual rep performance and it can also generate personalized training plans based on each rep's weak areas.

speaker: prospect
text: Whoa, that's a lot. I couldn't follow all of that.

speaker: rep
text: Sorry about that. Let me back up. The core value prop is this: we help new SDRs ramp 40% faster by giving them AI-powered coaching during live calls.

speaker: prospect
text: That's more interesting. How does real-time coaching work without being intrusive to the prospect?

speaker: rep
text: Great question. The coaching appears only on the rep's screen as subtle prompts. The prospect never sees or hears anything. It's like having a manager whispering tips in their ear.

speaker: prospect
text: Okay, that makes sense. We tried recording calls with Gong but the feedback is always after the fact.

speaker: rep
text: Exactly. Post-call review is valuable but you can't fix a mistake that already happened. Real-time coaching catches issues as they occur.

speaker: rep
text: And the scoring system helps managers identify exactly where each rep needs improvement instead of doing random call reviews.

speaker: rep
text: Plus the practice mode lets reps rehearse tough scenarios like objection handling and pricing conversations before they face them live.

speaker: rep
text: We also integrate with your existing call recording tools so you don't have to rip and replace anything.

speaker: prospect
text: Alright, you're doing the feature dump thing again. Let me ask you something specific.

speaker: rep
text: Sure, go ahead.

speaker: prospect
text: What does this cost and how quickly can we see results? Those are my two questions.

speaker: rep
text: Pricing starts at $89 per seat per month. Most teams our size go with the growth tier at $149 which includes the real-time coaching and practice modes. Results-wise, our customers typically see new rep ramp time drop from 90 days to about 55 days.

speaker: prospect
text: Hmm, that's not cheap when we're about to hire 15 reps. But the ramp time reduction would save us money.

speaker: rep
text: Right. If each rep generates about $50K in pipeline per month once ramped, getting them productive 35 days sooner is worth roughly $58K per rep. The tool pays for itself in the first month.

speaker: prospect
text: Fair point. Look, I need to jump to another call, but can you send me some info? I'd be open to a proper demo next week.

speaker: rep
text: Absolutely. I'll send over a one-pager and a case study from a company in your space. Would Tuesday or Wednesday work for a 30-minute demo?

speaker: prospect
text: Wednesday morning works. Send me a calendar invite.

speaker: rep
text: Will do. Wednesday morning it is. Thanks for your time, Alex.
